annotations.label: Discrete calculus

anchor.snippet: is the mathematical study of incremental change

annotations.note: An overarching discipline name, not a formalizable mathematical statement, though its core operator fwdDiff exists.

annotations.label: Discrete differential calculus

anchor.snippet: is the study of the definition, properties, and applications of the difference quotient

annotations.note: A meta-description of a sub-field rather than a definition Mathlib would state.

annotations.label: Difference quotient

anchor.snippet: is the rate of change of the function over one of the intervals

annotations.note: `slope f a b = (f b - f a) / (b - a)` is exactly the difference quotient over the interval [a,b].

annotations.label: Difference quotient as velocity

anchor.snippet: the difference quotient represents change with respect to time

annotations.note: A physical interpretation/example, not a formal statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Slope of a linear function

anchor.snippet: If a function is linear (that is, if the points of the graph of the function lie on a straight line)

annotations.note: The `slope` operator is defined but I found no dedicated lemma asserting it is constant for an affine/linear function.

annotations.label: Slope of the line between two points

anchor.snippet: The slope of the line between these two points

annotations.note: `slope f a b` is precisely the slope of the line through (a, f a) and (b, f b).

annotations.label: Difference quotient of the squaring function

anchor.snippet: Here is a particular example, the difference quotient of the squaring function

annotations.note: A worked example computation, not a named declaration; `fwdDiff` of x^2 is derivable but not stated.

annotations.label: Second difference quotient

anchor.snippet: is called the second difference quotient

annotations.note: Iterated forward differences `Δ_[h]^[2]` exist, but not the divided second difference quotient as such.

annotations.label: Discrete integral calculus

anchor.snippet: is the study of the definitions, properties, and applications of the Riemann sums

annotations.note: A meta-description of a sub-field rather than a formal definition.

annotations.label: Distance traveled in a given time

anchor.snippet: A motivating example is the distances traveled in a given time

annotations.note: A motivating physical example, not a formal statement.

annotations.label: Riemann sum

anchor.snippet: in sigma notation is

annotations.note: `integralSum` is the Riemann sum over a tagged (pre)partition, generalized to n-dimensional boxes.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus (statement)

anchor.snippet: states that differentiation and integration are inverse operations

annotations.note: The classical continuous FTC (integral of a derivative equals the difference of endpoint values) is fully formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of discrete calculus

anchor.snippet: is defined on a partition of the interval

annotations.note: `sum_range_sub` is the telescoping identity ∑ (f(i+1) - f i) = f n - f 0, the discrete analogue of the FTC.

annotations.label: Difference equation

anchor.snippet: Difference equations relate an unknown function to its difference or difference quotient

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes linear recurrences (a special case of difference equations) but not general difference equations.

annotations.label: Difference (coboundary) operator

anchor.snippet: The difference (or the exterior derivative , or the coboundary operator) of the function is given by

annotations.note: `fwdDiff h f n = f (n + h) - f n` is exactly the forward difference operator Δ.

annotations.label: Sum of a cochain

anchor.snippet: Then its sum is a function

annotations.note: Summation is available via `Finset.sum`, but there is no dedicated antidifference operator defined as the inverse of `fwdDiff`.

annotations.label: Constant rule (differences/sums)

anchor.snippet: These are their properties: Constant rule

annotations.note: `fwdDiff_const` states Δ of a constant function is zero.

annotations.label: Linearity (differences/sums)

anchor.snippet: Linearity : if

annotations.note: `fwdDiff_add` and `fwdDiff_const_smul` give additivity and scalar-linearity of Δ.

annotations.label: Product rule (differences/sums)

anchor.snippet: Product rule :

annotations.note: `fwdDiff_smul` is the discrete Leibniz rule Δ(f•g) = Δf•g + f•Δg + Δf•Δg.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus I (differences/sums)

anchor.snippet: Fundamental theorem of calculus I :

annotations.note: Summing a forward difference telescopes to the difference of endpoint values via `sum_range_sub`.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus II (differences/sums)

anchor.snippet: Fundamental theorem of calculus II :

annotations.note: The inverse direction (Δ of a partial-sum function recovers the summand) is derivable from telescoping but not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Exterior derivative on a graph

anchor.snippet: then its exterior derivative (or the differential) is the difference

annotations.note: Mathlib has only the unsigned `SimpleGraph.incMatrix`, not the signed edge differential df(e)=f(head)-f(tail).

annotations.label: Path integral of a cochain on a graph

anchor.snippet: then its integral over a sequence of edges

annotations.note: Walks/paths exist in `SimpleGraph`, but there is no integral of an edge cochain along a path.

annotations.label: Constant rule (graphs)

anchor.snippet: These are the properties: Constant rule

annotations.note: No graph exterior-derivative operator is formalized, so its constant rule is absent.

annotations.label: Linearity (graphs)

anchor.snippet: Linearity : if

annotations.note: No graph exterior-derivative operator is formalized, so its linearity is absent.

annotations.label: Product rule (graphs)

anchor.snippet: Product rule :

annotations.note: No graph exterior-derivative operator is formalized, so its product rule is absent.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus I (graphs)

anchor.snippet: consists of the edges

annotations.note: The path-integral version of the FTC on graphs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of calculus II (graphs)

anchor.snippet: if the graph is a tree

annotations.note: The tree/exactness version of the graph FTC is not formalized.

annotations.label: Simplicial complex

anchor.snippet: is a set of simplices that satisfies the following conditions

annotations.note: `Geometry.SimplicialComplex` (and an abstract version in AlgebraicTopology) formalizes simplicial complexes.

annotations.label: Orientation of a simplex

anchor.snippet: an orientation of a k -simplex is given by an ordering of the vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib's simplicial complexes carry no orientation/vertex-ordering equivalence-class structure.

annotations.label: Simplicial k-chain

anchor.snippet: A simplicial k -chain is a finite formal sum

annotations.note: Chain groups of simplicial objects exist via the alternating face map complex, but not formal sums of faces of a geometric simplicial complex.

annotations.label: Vector space of k-chains

anchor.snippet: The vector space of k -chains on

annotations.note: The degree-k object of a (singular/simplicial) chain complex realizes this, but not as the free module on geometric k-simplices.

annotations.label: Boundary operator

anchor.snippet: is the linear operator defined by

annotations.note: `objD` is the simplicial boundary ∂ = Σ(-1)^i dᵢ; the general differential is `HomologicalComplex.d`.

annotations.label: Cycles and boundaries

anchor.snippet: are referred to as cycles , and the subgroup

annotations.note: Cycles (ker ∂) and boundaries (im ∂) are formalized for arbitrary homological complexes.

annotations.label: Boundary of a boundary is zero

anchor.snippet: this says that the boundary of anything has no boundary

annotations.note: `d_comp_d` proves ∂∘∂ = 0 generally; `AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared` gives the simplicial case.

annotations.label: Elementary interval

anchor.snippet: An elementary interval is a subset

annotations.note: Cubical complexes and their elementary intervals are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Elementary cube

anchor.snippet: is the finite product of elementary intervals

annotations.note: Elementary cubes (products of elementary intervals) are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cubical complex

anchor.snippet: is a cubical complex if it can be written as a union of elementary cubes

annotations.note: Mathlib has no cubical complex structure.

annotations.label: Chain complex

anchor.snippet: connected by linear operators (called boundary operators )

annotations.note: `ChainComplex` (a `HomologicalComplex` with the descending complex shape) is fully formalized.

annotations.label: Simplicial map

anchor.snippet: A simplicial map is a map between simplicial complexes

annotations.note: `PreAbstractSimplicialComplex.map` pushes faces forward along a vertex map, but a bundled simplicial-map type is not defined.

annotations.label: Chain map

anchor.snippet: between two chain complexes

annotations.note: Morphisms of homological/chain complexes (commuting with differentials) are formalized as `HomologicalComplex.Hom`.

annotations.label: Chain map preserves cycles and boundaries

anchor.snippet: A chain map sends cycles to cycles and boundaries to boundaries

annotations.note: Functoriality is realized by induced maps on cycles/homology (e.g. `cyclesMap`), implementing that chain maps respect cycles and boundaries.

annotations.label: Dual (coboundary) operator

anchor.snippet: we consider its dual space

annotations.note: The coboundary appears as the differential of a `CochainComplex` (e.g. dual of a chain complex), though not packaged as an explicit transpose of ∂ here.

annotations.label: Cochain complex

anchor.snippet: is the dual notion to a chain complex

annotations.note: `CochainComplex` (ascending-index `HomologicalComplex`) is fully formalized.

annotations.label: Degree (dimension)

anchor.snippet: is referred to as the degree (or dimension )

annotations.note: The grading index of a complex exists, but 'degree/dimension' of a cochain is not a separately named notion.

annotations.label: Cochains, cocycles, coboundaries

anchor.snippet: are called cochains

annotations.note: Cochains, cocycles, and coboundaries are formalized abstractly (`HomologicalComplex.cycles`) and concretely in group cohomology.

annotations.label: Poincaré lemma

anchor.snippet: The Poincaré lemma states that if

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the continuous Poincaré lemma for closed 1-forms on convex sets, not the discrete cochain/contractible-complex version.

annotations.label: Discrete exterior derivative on forms

anchor.snippet: When we refer to cochains as discrete (differential) forms

annotations.note: The discrete exterior derivative is the cochain coboundary (a `CochainComplex` differential), but it is not packaged under a 'discrete differential form' interface.

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem

anchor.snippet: Stokes' theorem is a statement about the discrete differential forms on manifolds

annotations.note: Mathlib has the continuous divergence (Stokes-type) theorem, but not the discrete duality ⟨dω, c⟩ = ⟨ω, ∂c⟩ for cochains.

annotations.label: Wedge product

anchor.snippet: this is a construction that creates from forms higher order forms

annotations.note: The algebraic wedge product exists on exterior algebras/powers and alternating maps, but not for discrete differential forms (cochains).

annotations.label: Wedge product on cubical complexes

anchor.snippet: For cubical complexes , the wedge product is defined on every cube

annotations.note: Cubical complexes are absent from Mathlib, so the cube-wise wedge product is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cup product

anchor.snippet: the wedge product is implemented as the cup product

annotations.note: No cup product on cochains/cohomology is currently defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Coboundary of a cup product (Leibniz rule)

anchor.snippet: The coboundary of the cup product of cochains

annotations.note: Without a cup product, its Leibniz/coboundary identity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cup product of cocycles

anchor.snippet: The cup product of two cocycles is again a cocycle

annotations.note: No cup product exists, so this closure property is absent.

annotations.label: Graded-commutativity of the cup product

anchor.snippet: The cup product operation satisfies the identity

annotations.note: No cup product exists, so its graded-commutativity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplace operator (informal)

anchor.snippet: is (up to a factor) the rate at which the average value

annotations.note: The discrete graph Laplacian (degree minus adjacency, comparing a value to its neighbors' average) is formalized as `lapMatrix`.

annotations.label: Codifferential

anchor.snippet: is an operator defined on

annotations.note: No codifferential δ (adjoint of the exterior derivative) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Codifferential is adjoint of exterior derivative

anchor.snippet: The codifferential is the adjoint of the exterior derivative

annotations.note: Without a codifferential, this adjointness statement is absent.

annotations.label: Codifferential squared is zero

anchor.snippet: the codifferential has the corresponding property

annotations.note: Without a codifferential, δ∘δ = 0 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplace operator (definition)

anchor.snippet: The Laplace operator is defined by

annotations.note: The graph Laplacian L = D - A is formalized as `lapMatrix`, but the Hodge Laplacian dδ + δd on cochains is not.
